title: The Star Entertainment QLD Limited — Systemic failures
investigation_objective: AUSTRAC commenced civil penalty proceedings in the Federal Court against The Star Entertainment QLD Limited alleging serious and systemic non-compliance with Australia's anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing (AML/CTF) laws, including poor governance, failures of risk management, and failure to carry out appropriate ongoing customer due diligence over a number of years. The investigation followed a proactive AUSTRAC industry-wide compliance campaign beginning September 2019, with an enforcement investigation opened in June 2021. As of March 2026, the Federal Court had not yet delivered judgment.
scope: Subject of Federal Court civil penalty proceedings brought by AUSTRAC alleging serious and systemic AML/CTF non-compliance; Alleged failures include poor governance, inadequate risk management, and failure to conduct ongoing customer due diligence over a number of years; Proceedings remain undetermined as of March 2026, more than four years after the enforcement investigation was opened
